notes: The embedded MBID resolves to a DIFFERENT Chet Baker compilation titled 'Chet Baker' (EmArcy, tracklist Summertime/Girl from Greenland/etc.), so MB does not entity-match our 'Live in Rosenheim'. However evidence.websearch (resolved=true; jazzdisco.org + Jazz Messengers + AllMusic) entity-matches and confirms: 'Live in Rosenheim' recorded 17 Apr 1988 in Rosenheim, released 1989 on Timeless Records. Year 1989 correct, label Timeless correct. All 5 keyTracks (Funk in Deep Freeze, I'm a Fool to Want You, Portrait in Black and White, In a Sentimental Mood, If I Should Lose You) verified by websearch findings.tracklist. albumDNA accurate. keyTracks matched against websearch (MB tracklist discarded as wrong entity).

MB search match (sim 1.0): Jazz Street, 1989, Timeless Records, credited Jaco Pastorius + Brian Melvin; all 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. albumDNA (jazz fusion, 1989, Timeless Records, No Slack/Jazz Street) fully consistent. ours.wikipediaUrl is null. The evidence carries a stale stored_url to 'Red Rose Speedway' (Paul McCartney/Wings) which is the wrong entity; appears already cleared to null in current data.

MB embedded_mbid: self-titled David Sanborn, Warner Bros. Records, all 5 keyTracks present; tracklist incl. 'I Do It for Your Love' (Paul Simon) matches albumDNA. Wiki page (David Sanborn (album)) entity-matches: 'Sanborn ... released by Warner Bros. in 1976 ... version of I Do It for Your Love by Paul Simon.' Year tension: prose makes no year claim; field year=1990 matches the MB embedded release (DE), while the album was first issued 1976 (wiki). Consistent with this dataset's convention of cataloguing a later release/edition; not flagged as error.

fieldIssues.evidence: Karl‐Heinz Steffens, David Gazarov, Martin Zehn, Keith Copeland, Chris Lachotta (performers); 'Parker' appears only among composer credits (Yardbird Suite)
fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: artists: Brubeck, Debussy, Donato, Gilberto, Evans, Jones, Honegger, Parker, Poulenc, Stravinsky, Karl‐Heinz Steffens, David Gazarov, Martin Zehn, Keith Copeland, Chris Lachotta; date 1997

wrongClaims.problem: This 1997 Tudor album is a clarinet recital (performed by Karl‐Heinz Steffens et al.), not a Charlie Parker album. Parker appears only as composer of one track ('Yardbird Suite') and died in 1955; he cannot be the artist of a 1997 release.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: date 1997 ... artists: ... Honegger, Parker, Poulenc, Stravinsky, Karl‐Heinz Steffens, David Gazarov ... tracks: ... Yardbird Suite ...

notes: albumDNA prose is actually CORRECT about the real album (a 1997 Tudor clarinet recital: Blue Rondo a la Turk / Debussy Premiere rhapsodie / Waltz for Debby / Clarinet Sonatina), and all 5 keyTracks match MB. The error is the artist FIELD = Charlie Parker (a composer credit pulled from the tracklist), not the prose. NOTE: this is the same MB release (MBID 505e34e7) as item 'blue-rondo-brubeck' -- two duplicate catalog entries of one clarinet-recital album mis-filed under two composer names. Repair: dedupe and set artist to the actual performer (Karl-Heinz Steffens).

fieldIssues.evidence: Karl‐Heinz Steffens, David Gazarov, Martin Zehn, Keith Copeland, Chris Lachotta (performers); 'Brubeck' appears only among composer credits (Blue Rondo a la Turk)
fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: artists: Brubeck, Debussy, Donato, Gilberto, Evans, Jones, Honegger, Parker, Poulenc, Stravinsky, Karl‐Heinz Steffens, David Gazarov, Martin Zehn, Keith Copeland, Chris Lachotta; date 1997

wrongClaims.problem: This 1997 Tudor album is a clarinet recital performed by Karl‐Heinz Steffens et al., not a Dave Brubeck album. Brubeck appears only as composer of 'Blue Rondo a la Turk'.

notes: Same MB release (MBID 505e34e7) as item 'blue-rondo'. albumDNA correctly describes the clarinet recital and all 6 keyTracks match MB; only the artist FIELD (Dave Brubeck, a composer credit) is wrong. Repair: dedupe with 'blue-rondo' and set artist to the actual performer (Karl-Heinz Steffens).

Entity is solid: MB search (sim 1.0) confirms Forever Love, Mark Whitfield, Verve (533 921-2), 1997-03-04; all 5 keyTracks present; albumDNA (jazz guitarist, Verve, 1997, ballads/standards, My One and Only Love, Nature Boy) all consistent. The one substantive claim that cannot be checked against pack evidence is 'vocalist Diana Krall guests on a few tracks' -- MB tracklist carries no personnel and the stored_url 'Thomas Whitfield (singer)' is a different gospel artist (wrong entity, not shipped). Absence is not contradiction; the Krall guesting claim is unverifiable from the evidence here (not refuted).

MB search (sim 1.0): In Their Own Sweet Way, Dave Brubeck, Telarc Jazz (CD-83355), 1997; all 5 keyTracks present. Wiki page entity-matches and confirms the prose nearly verbatim: 'Dave Brubeck and his quintet ... accompanied by his four sons on a recording for the first time.' Minor inconsistency: wiki extract says '1998' while its short-description and MB say 1997; our 1997 is supported by MB and the wiki short-desc, so not flagged.

Entity confirmed: MB embedded_mbid gives Tenor Titans, Storyville Records (STCD 8288), 1997, credited Dexter Gordon + Ben Webster; all 5 keyTracks present. albumDNA names only Ben Webster (editorial; album is a Gordon/Webster split). The substantive uncheckable claim is the explicit 'Recorded for Storyville Records in 1997' -- MB carries only a 1997 RELEASE date, not a recording date, so this recording-year assertion can be neither confirmed nor refuted from pack evidence. (Background knowledge that Webster's recordings predate 1997 is not citable here.) ours.wikipediaUrl null; stored_url 'Tomasz Szukalski' is a different Polish saxophonist (wrong entity, not shipped).

Entity confirmed: MB search (sim 1.0) gives Leon Thomas in Berlin, Leon Thomas, releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 2000 (matched release is a 2002 BMG France reissue); all 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. Substantive claims that cannot be checked against pack evidence: (a) 'with the saxophonist/arranger Oliver Nelson' -- MB lists no personnel; (b) 'recorded in 1970 in Berlin' -- MB carries no recording date; (c) label 'Flying Dutchman' vs MB's reissue label 'BMG France' -- MB's 2002 reissue does not refute Flying Dutchman as the original label (absence is not contradiction). No contradictions, but multiple substantive personnel/date claims are unverifiable. opensearch returned no wiki page; ours.wikipediaUrl null.
